skill.name: para-skill
skill.description: Classify, route, normalize, review, and archive Obsidian notes using the PARA method. The skill provides decision logic for Projects, Areas, Resources, and Archive workflows while preserving existing markdown and frontmatter conventions.
skill.summary: Apply PARA decision logic to organize and review Obsidian vault notes.
skill.author: scifishelf

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, network, and weak cryptography indicators, but review found these are Markdown backticks, documentation links, badges, dates, and examples rather than executable code. No prompt injection, malicious exfiltration, or hidden execution behavior was found. The remaining risk is low because the skill can guide filesystem changes in an Obsidian vault, mitigated by dry-run defaults, explicit authorization rules, and no-deletion guidance.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Operations Require User Oversight
low_findings.description: The skill can route, move, archive, and update frontmatter for Obsidian notes. This is a legitimate capability, but publication should disclose that it may change vault files when the user authorizes action.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines explicitly describe routing, archiving, and fallback direct file writes. Safety rules reduce severity because the skill defaults to dry-run behavior and requires confirmation for sensitive moves.

low_findings.title: Static Command, Network, and Crypto Findings Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: Ruby shell execution, network, and weak-cryptography indicators are caused by Markdown inline code, fenced examples, badges, dates, public documentation links, and an installation command. No executable implementation code, cryptographic use, or automatic network call was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is Markdown documentation, prose, and examples, not code that the skill executes. The files reviewed contain no scripts, handlers, cryptography calls, or runtime logic that would execute these strings.

content.value_statement: Obsidian vaults often become hard to review when notes mix projects, responsibilities, references, and old material. This skill applies PARA rules to classify notes, propose safe routing, normalize metadata, and surface cleanup actions.
actual_capabilities: Classifies notes as Projects, Areas, Resources, or Archive using documented decision rules.
Suggests target folders for notes based on PARA category and existing vault conventions.
Drafts PARA frontmatter fields while preserving non-PARA metadata and tags.
Produces hygiene review reports for inbox backlog, stale areas, projects, resources, and archive candidates.
Supports defensive archiving with dry-run output, archive metadata, and changelog guidance.
Coordinates with Obsidian-focused skills for markdown formatting and file operations.
limitations: It provides decision logic, not a standalone Obsidian plugin or vault database.
It needs access to note content or file listings to classify existing vault items accurately.
Ambiguous notes are marked for review instead of forced into a category.
Bulk moves and destructive edits are outside the intended workflow.

use_cases.title: Process a messy inbox
use_cases.description: Classify uncategorized inbox notes and propose where each item belongs in a PARA vault.

use_cases.title: Prepare a periodic vault review
use_cases.description: Generate a structured review of stale projects, active areas, obsolete resources, and archive candidates.

use_cases.title: Standardize team note metadata
use_cases.description: Draft consistent PARA frontmatter without overwriting existing fields, links, or tags.

prompt_templates.title: Classify one note
prompt_templates.prompt: Classify this note using PARA. Explain the category, confidence level, and two supporting signals before suggesting any move.

prompt_templates.title: Route an inbox item
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this inbox note and suggest the best PARA folder. Show the target path and frontmatter draft, but do not move it yet.

prompt_templates.title: Run a vault hygiene review
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a PARA hygiene review for this vault. Prioritize inbox backlog, projects without next steps, stale areas, and archive candidates.

prompt_templates.title: Normalize metadata safely
prompt_templates.prompt: Normalize PARA frontmatter for these notes. Preserve all existing non-PARA fields, mark uncertain items needs_review, and show a change log.

output_examples.input: A note about preparing an April leadership workshop with open tasks and a target date.
output_examples.output: Classified as Project with high confidence. The output explains the deadline and open tasks, then proposes a Projects path and minimal PARA metadata.

output_examples.input: A folder review request for a vault with many inbox notes and old completed projects.
output_examples.output: Produces a review summary covering inbox backlog, projects, areas, resources, archive candidates, open decisions, and prioritized recommendations.

output_examples.input: A reference note with existing tags and non-PARA frontmatter.
output_examples.output: Suggests Resource classification and adds only PARA-specific metadata while preserving the existing tags and custom fields.

best_practices: Start with dry-run mode and review proposed moves before applying them.
Provide current folder paths, note titles, frontmatter, and relevant body text for better classification.
Use needs_review for ambiguous notes instead of forcing a category.
anti_patterns: Do not classify notes by topic alone without checking current action relevance.
Do not run bulk moves without explicit user approval and a reviewed change list.
Do not overwrite existing metadata, tags, or wikilinks while adding PARA fields.

faq.question: Does this skill edit Obsidian files by itself?
faq.answer: It mainly provides decisions and drafts. File changes should happen only after explicit user approval or delegated tool action.

faq.question: Can it work outside Obsidian?
faq.answer: Yes, the PARA logic can apply to markdown-based systems, but paths and metadata conventions may need adjustment.

faq.question: How does it handle uncertain notes?
faq.answer: It sets low confidence, marks the note for review, and explains the competing classification signals.

faq.question: Will it delete old notes?
faq.answer: No. The documented workflow says archive means move, never delete.

faq.question: Can it preserve existing frontmatter?
faq.answer: Yes. It is designed to add or update PARA fields while leaving unrelated fields unchanged.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It can coordinate with Obsidian markdown or CLI skills when available.
